What to Measure: Count unique fields returned per record. Check for consumer + professional categories. Look at field taxonomy and typing.
Calculate match rate by segment: work email, personal email, phone, name+company. Note if provider rejects certain identifier types.
For each record: populated fields / total fields. Break down by category. Flag stale data.
Check for normalized job titles, enumerated values, consistent date/phone/address formats. Note free-text vs. typed fields.
Check for financial, property, vehicle, interest, household, political, charitable data. Count distinct consumer categories with 10+ populated fields.
Determine if graph is deterministic, probabilistic, or lookup. Test with outdated identifiers. Check for multi-identifier linking and confidence scores.
Calculate effective cost per enriched record at expected volume. Check for annual minimums, overage pricing, failed lookup charges.
Verify SOC 2, CCPA/GDPR compliance, named data sources, third-party audits, published DPA, opt-out mechanisms.
